meta.name: Soul Food Sweet Potato Pie
meta.author: SSA Canonical Catalog

source.notes: Canonical SSA soul food baseline. Classic sweet potato pie with a smooth custard filling and warm spices. Uses a single 9-inch crust (homemade or store-bought).

meta.summary: Silky sweet potato custard pie with cinnamon, nutmeg, and vanilla in a flaky 9-inch crust.

notes: Custard pies are safely cooked when the center reaches at least 165°F.
Pie should jiggle slightly in the center and set as it cools.

ingredients.label: Sweet potatoes, cooked and mashed (about 2–3 medium)

steps.title: Preheat oven
steps.body: Preheat oven to 350°F. Place a sheet pan on the lower rack if desired to catch any drips.

steps.title: Prepare sweet potato mash
steps.body: If not already cooked: bake sweet potatoes at 400°F until very tender (45–70 minutes), cool slightly, peel, and mash until smooth. Measure 2 cups mashed sweet potato.

steps.title: Mix filling
steps.body: In a bowl, combine mashed sweet potato, melted butter, brown sugar, granulated sugar, cinnamon, nutmeg, ginger (optional), salt, vanilla, and lemon juice (optional). Mix until smooth.

steps.title: Add eggs and milk
steps.body: Whisk in eggs until fully incorporated. Slowly whisk in evaporated milk until the filling is smooth and pourable.

steps.title: Fill crust
steps.body: Place pie crust in a 9-inch pie plate. Pour filling into crust and smooth the top.

steps.title: Bake until set
steps.body: Bake 50–65 minutes, until the edges are set and the center jiggles slightly (like gelatin). If crust browns too fast, cover edges loosely with foil.

steps.title: Cool to set
steps.body: Cool at room temperature 1–2 hours to fully set. Slice and serve (plain, with whipped cream, or a drizzle of caramel).
